danger_signals.visual_form: Kirby series enemy characters positioned on course
danger_signals.meaning: Obstacles to overcome; targets to hit for progression and power-ups

danger_signals.visual_form: Multiple distinct enemy sprites scattered across isometric golf course
danger_signals.meaning: Course incomplete; must defeat all enemies before goal hole manifests

danger_signals.visual_form: Kirby in base form without visual transformation effects
danger_signals.meaning: Limited ability to destroy certain obstacles; inefficient course completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Final enemy transforms into visible hole sprite after all others defeated
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage available; course can be completed

safety_signals.visual_form: Kirby visually transforms (tornado, spark ball, UFO form, etc.)
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced capabilities unlocked; dangerous obstacles now passable

safety_signals.visual_form: Stars change to player color (pink or yellow) upon contact
safety_signals.meaning: Progress secured; points accumulated toward victory

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Course entry point; isometric golf tee position

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Identical to Kirby but yellow palette swap
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Multiplayer mode only; alternate starting position
Implied presence through stolen stars plot device

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinctive enemy sprites from Kirby universe
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each enemy provides unique Copy ability when hit
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scattered throughout courses; reposition per hole

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple enemy sprites visible on isometric terrain; goal hole absent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kirby must navigate and target enemies; collision mechanics active

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final enemy sprite transforms into goal hole visual; terrain cleared
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Goal becomes accessible; hole-in-one mechanics activate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Kirby sprite displays transformation (tornado swirl, spark particles, UFO silhouette)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enhanced movement; obstacle destruction possible; temporary ability window

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stars display player color (pink or yellow); ownership changes on contact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stars can be stolen; attack Copy abilities force opponent turn skip

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Medal award screen displays grade (gold, silver, bronze) based on performance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Medal tier unlocks alternative course versions; progression gating

environmental_storytelling.example: King Dedede steals stars from night sky; Kirby's quest to return them provides thematic link between golf course design and story
Kirby series enemies populate courses as both obstacles and power-up sources, maintaining franchise identity within sports context
Miniature golf course layout viewed from fixed isometric angle communicates spatial relationships and obstacle positioning at a glance
In multiplayer, star color shift (pink/yellow) visually tracks territorial control and scoring without UI overlay

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationary obstacles; defeated upon Kirby collision; final remaining enemy transforms into goal
Launched via power/angle/spin input; standard collision physics; no special obstacle interaction
Enhanced movement; spiral visual effect; wind mechanics affect trajectory
Destructible obstacle interaction; spark particle effects; selective barrier removal
Free movement mode; hover capability; temporary flight duration
Neutral positioning; neutral color; available for collection
Change to player color; locked to player ownership until stolen or round ends
Visible sprite appears; worth two stars in multiplayer; round-ending trigger

notes: Kirby's Dream Course uses classic mini-golf mechanics applied to Kirby franchise IP. World state communication relies heavily on sprite visibility/transformation, color coding in multiplayer, and enemy presence/absence as progression gates. No explicit danger/safety mechanics beyond obstacle avoidance and stroke economy. Faction communication is minimal—primarily Kirby vs. enemies, with Dedede as narrative-only antagonist. The game's visual language is straightforward: clarity of isometric layout, distinctive enemy sprites, and transformation effects convey all mechanical state. No environmental hazards or dynamic world-state shifts; all state changes are course-specific and deterministic.
